petitioner_background: The petitioner is an advertising agency seeking to classify the beneficiary, who serves as the company's Creative Director, as an individual of extraordinary ability in the arts under EB-1A. The beneficiary works in the field of Experience Design (XD), described as the art of designing products, processes, services, events and environments with a focus on user experience quality and culturally relevant solutions. He was extensively involved in a major advertising campaign recognized in the industry, and previously worked at a top design firm in Denmark. The Texas Service Center denied the petition, and the petitioner appealed.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence that the beneficiary was responsible for elements of an advertising campaign that won a number of awards, including what the petitioner characterized as the 'Oscars of the advertising world.' Evidence included online and print materials describing the festival as the world's biggest advertising festival. A letter from the Managing Partner and Global Chief Digital Officer verified the award 'is the single most prestigious awards festival within advertising and interaction design globally.' Additional evidence described the other award as tantamount to the Oscar in film, Tony in theater, Emmy in television, and Grammy in music, and a 1987 article calling it 'the most prestigious honor in the advertising business' and an April 2012 article calling it 'the [advertising] industry's most recognized competition honoring creative achievement.' An August 14, 2013 letter from the festival CEO verified the beneficiary's work 'has been recogni[z]ed at our festival,' with four awards. The beneficiary was credited as Associate Creative Director - interaction design. An article explained that jurors awarded the campaign and one other advertisement at a higher tier because they were equally strong as integrated works. The petitioner also earned additional awards in the Integrated category for the same advertisement. Reference letters from the Petitioner's Associate Director of Human Resources Operations and the Senior Vice President and Executive Creative Director described the beneficiary as having 'led his team in creating concepts' and that his 'design leadership proved critical.'
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found the awards were lesser nationally or internationally recognized prizes or awards in the field of advertising (not rising to the level of a one-time achievement/major internationally recognized award). The AAO applied a de facto honoree analysis, noting that it may consider on a case-by-case basis whether the beneficiary was so inextricably linked with the awarded entity that he is recognized in the field as the de facto honoree. Based on the CEO letter confirming the campaign was recognized with four awards, the beneficiary's credited role as Associate Creative Director - interaction design, and the jury's reasoning that it awarded the campaign because of its integrated/interactive nature (for which the beneficiary was responsible), combined with reference letters establishing his major and significant role, the AAO found the beneficiary was recognized in the field as one of the de facto honorees. The criterion was met.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence of the beneficiary's involvement in the advertising campaign, including published materials confirming its significant impact on the advertising field. Evidence included a statement from the Executive Creative Director of another firm stating the award-winning campaign 'expands the idea of what creative agencies can do.' A leading advertising and marketing trade publication named the campaign as a top 15 advertising campaign of the 21st century; another publication listed it as the second most influential campaign of the 21st century in a January 2015 article; another trade publication named it the digital campaign of the decade. A statement from the Petitioner's Executive Vice President and Global Chief Creative Officer indicated the beneficiary was 'directly responsible' for the company's 'most successful product innovations.' Evidence showed the beneficiary led a team that created concepts for the platform and product, which merges digital and physical experiences, as well as its associated advertising campaign. Reference letters confirmed the beneficiary's design leadership was critical to winning 'more than 25 international accolades,' and a UX Lead and Manager / former Vice President of Product Design noted the beneficiary 'led the Experience Design of the project to great commercial success and critical acclaim.'
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO applied the standard that contributions must be both original (first person or one of the first to have done the work) and of major significance (significantly advanced the field as a whole), citing Silverman v. Eastrich Multiple Investor Fund. The AAO found the published materials confirmed the advertising campaign significantly impacted the advertising field, with trade publications naming it among the top advertising campaigns of the 21st century and the digital campaign of the decade. Because the beneficiary was extensively involved in the campaign, specifically with the interactive/integrated aspects for which it was recognized, and directly responsible for the company's most successful product innovations, the AAO concluded the petitioner had documented original contributions of major significance. The criterion was met.

evidence_per_criterion.evidence_submitted: The petitioner submitted evidence that in 2009 the petitioner advertising agency won an award, along with articles published about the company in various publications indicating the publication's standing in the advertising field. Materials showed the agency 'cemented its reputation for melding technology and creative' and that its 'work did not merely reflect popular culture; it steered it.' The beneficiary currently serves as Creative Director. Reference evidence stated the beneficiary was 'directly responsible for some of [the Petitioner's] most successful product innovations' and 'helped [it] to win a remarkable 18 awards for [its] preeminent account.' As Creative Director, the beneficiary 'direct[s] the creative development, design, and implementation of interactive digital marketing campaigns and transactional experiences for [the company's] Fortune 500 clients.'
evidence_per_criterion.aao_analysis: The AAO found the record confirmed the petitioner is an organization or establishment with a distinguished reputation based on the published articles, industry recognition, and description of the agency's reputation for melding technology and creative work. The AAO then found the beneficiary performed a leading and critical role based on his current position as Creative Director, his direct responsibility for the company's most successful product innovations, his contributions helping win 18 awards for a preeminent account, and his role directing creative development for Fortune 500 clients. The criterion was met.

outcome_reasoning: In the final merits determination, the AAO found the beneficiary was closely involved in an advertising campaign that won numerous prestigious accolades specifically recognizing the integrated/interactive elements for which he was responsible, including multiple additional awards beyond those initially discussed. Testimony verified the campaign 'nearly single-handedly pioneered a new consumer category: wearable computing and the integration of software and mobile apps into our daily lives,' and top company executives confirmed the beneficiary was integral to the project's creation and commercial success. The AAO additionally considered his prior work at a top design firm in Denmark (described as Europe's largest strategic design firm and third-ranking international strategic design firm in the world), where he was instrumental in the company's rising status. Numerous reference letters from industry experts attested to the beneficiary's top status in the field, with experts stating he ranks 'at the very top' of the Experience Design field and represents 'truly best-in-class talent.' The AAO concluded the record in the aggregate confirmed the beneficiary enjoys a level of expertise consistent with being one of the small percentage at the very top of the field with sustained national or international acclaim.
legal_citations: Rijal v. USCIS, 772 F. Supp. 2d 1339 (W.D. Wash. 2011)
Silverman v. Eastrich Multiple Investor Fund, L.P., 51 F.3d 28 (3d Cir. 1995)
Matter of Price, 20 I&N Dec. 953 (Act. Assoc. Comm'r 1994)
